emergent_themes.description: Ordering patterns directly tied to American football training and game schedules, creating predictable high-convenience moments
Ongoing reliance on parents for grocery ordering and likely broader financial support despite living independently
Dramatic shift in delivery accessibility and habits when moving from rural to urban university environment

coding_summary: P015 demonstrates highly contextual ordering behavior driven primarily by sports schedule fatigue and social influence from teammates. His delivery habits are fundamentally shaped by convenience needs after training/games rather than planned indulgence. The diary data reveals more restrained actual ordering behavior (1 order in 7 days) compared to his interview claims (1-2 per week), suggesting either overestimation or an atypical diary week. Key patterns include strong parental dependency for grocery logistics, platform loyalty driven by subscription benefits and familiarity, and clear post-graduation expectations of increased ordering frequency with higher income. His rural background creates a strong appreciation for urban delivery convenience that wasn't previously available.

coverage_gaps.reason: Participant orders solo by default and shows no evidence of stigma around this behavior - operates independently from flatmates
